title: Seattle Just Broke a World Record With 830 People Eating Dim Sum
script: The biggest dim sum ever! Seattle's Chinatown-International District gathered 830 people on a closed block of South King Street and broke the Guinness World Record for the world's largest dim sum gathering. The previous record was 764, set in Sydney in 2019. To qualify, everyone had to stay seated simultaneously — 900 showed up, but 70 stood up before the headcount and disqualified themselves. The dumplings came from Jade Garden restaurant, whose team hand-rolled 3,600 by hand. At 3:32 p.m., the Guinness adjudicator made it official. Seattle won. The neighborhood has dealt with years of safety concerns and gentrification pressure — and it answered with 830 people eating dumplings together in the street. How many of those 70 people who stood up early are regretting it right now?
